what: Defining and structure of cross-situational learning (XSL) datasets.
title: Overview of `XSLmodels`: Importing cross-situational datasets and fitting models
text: Whether already human data experiment like fit models , like simply evaluate model performance given experiment’s training trials, vignette show 1) structure training trials, 2) examine structure experiment, 3) run model(s) training trials, 4) specify model(s) tested.

previous_headings: Defining and structure of cross-situational learning (XSL) datasets.
title: Overview of `XSLmodels`: Importing cross-situational datasets and fitting models
text: Cross-situational word learning (XSL) datasets defined xslData class, requires defining minimally training phase testing phase. trial training phase presents participants (models) one words one referents (.e. objects). package, word ww object oo referred index (.e., wiw_i, ojo_j), --learned word-object pairs assumed index (.e. wi=oiw_i = o_i). Thus, dataset defines list train words objects indices trial, also defined lists (allow potentially varying number words/objects per trial). test trials defined list available objects test trial. dataset can also given short label, longer (maybe per-item?) condition. already collected sample human participants, can optionally include per-item accuracy, vector length equal number --learned items) number participants (n_subj). sample size accuracy can compared model performance, desired, used fit model parameters. show define new dataset.
code: xslData(train = list(words = list(c(1, 2), c(2, 3)),                      objects = list(c(1, 2), c(2, 3))),         test = list(words = list(1, 2, 3),                     objects = list(1:3, 1:3, 1:3)),         label = "example dataset",         condition = "example condition") #> xslData object with label "example dataset" and condition "example condition" #>   training trials: 2 #>       test trials: 3 #>             words: 3 #>           objects: 3 #>        accuracies: 0

previous_headings: Defining and structure of cross-situational learning (XSL) datasets.
title: Overview of `XSLmodels`: Importing cross-situational datasets and fitting models
text: get_example_ambiguous_condition() get_example_unambiguous_condition() return simple example experimental conditions, consist list training trials, optional vector perf containing per-word performance people test trials, optional list test trials, enumerating --tested word set referents presented choosing among. list training trials composed list words presented trial, list referents (objs) presented trial. Shown , example ambiguous condition three training trials (ag$trials). first trial words 1 2 appear (ag$trials$words[[1]]) alongside referents 1 2 (ag$trials$objs[[1]]; order words/referents within trial irrelevant).
code: ag <- get_example_ambiguous_condition() ag #> xslData object with label "example condition" and condition "ambiguous" #>   training trials: 2 #>       test trials: 0 #>             words: 4 #>           objects: 4 #>        accuracies: 4

title: Overview of `XSLmodels`: Importing cross-situational datasets and fitting models
text: One common way summarize structure cross-situational word learning experiment make matrix word-object co-occurrences, can visualized heatmap show strength association word object. create_cooc_matrix() return word x object matrix showing tallied co-occurrences word object across training trials.

previous_headings: Defining and structure of cross-situational learning (XSL) datasets.
title: Overview of `XSLmodels`: Importing cross-situational datasets and fitting models
text: dataset combining 53 experimental conditions included package xsl_datasets. example, summary one experimental condition dataset: asymmetric (3x4; .e. 3 words 4 objects per trial) condition 36 training trials, 18 words 18 objects corresponding mean accuracies 25 subjects. (Note test trials: 0 indicates condition used 18AFC test trials, referents given tested word. Conditions show subset trained word-referent pairs (e.g. 4AFC) specify list referents shown tested word.) properties condition can accessed examined detail:
code: xsl_datasets[[1]] #> xslData object with label "201" and condition "3x4" #>   training trials: 36 #>       test trials: 0 #>             words: 18 #>           objects: 18 #>        accuracies: 18 #>          subjects: 25 # properties of an experimental condition: names(xsl_datasets[[1]]) #> [1] "train"     "test"      "accuracy"  "n_subj"    "label"     "condition" # e.g., mean accuracy for each tested word (i.e., P(choosing correct referent | word)): xsl_datasets[[1]]$accuracy #>  [1] 0.28 0.12 0.16 0.32 0.08 0.08 0.36 0.16 0.04 0.12 0.20 0.40 0.44 0.20 0.12 #> [16] 0.08 0.20 0.08

text: Now definition structure datasets clear, turn running fitting models, finally discussing structure xslMod, extend existing model create new one.

title: Overview of `XSLmodels`: Importing cross-situational datasets and fitting models
code: run3 <- xsl_run(uncfam(X = .1, C = 1, B = .98), data = xsl_datasets[1:3]) run3$sse #> [1] 0.2854364

previous_headings: Running, fitting, and defining XSL models.
title: Overview of `XSLmodels`: Importing cross-situational datasets and fitting models
text: Models return word-object matrix associations (‘hypotheses’, .e. binary-valued associations), well conditional probability selecting intended referent, given word (.e., P(referent | word)).
code: # Test different models on a subset of datasets gt <- xsl_run(guess_and_test(f = .1, sa = .5), data = xsl_datasets[1:3]) gt$sse #> [1] 0.2530896  pt <- xsl_run(pursuit(gamma = .2, threshold = .3, lambda = .05), data = xsl_datasets[1:3]) pt$sse #> [1] 8.834818  # Compare with baseline bl <- xsl_run(baseline(), data = xsl_datasets[1:3]) bl$sse #> [1] 0.2306

title: Overview of `XSLmodels`: Importing cross-situational datasets and fitting models
text: package includes several types models: Association-based models: - uncfam() - Uncertainty familiarity biased model - fazly() - Fazly et al. model - rescorla_wagner() - Rescorla-Wagner model - bayesian_decay() - Bayesian decay model Sampling-based models: - uncfam_sampling() - Sampling version uncfam - multi_sampling() - Multi-hypothesis sampling - guess_and_test() - Guess test model - propose_but_verify() - Propose verify model - pursuit() - Pursuit model Baseline models: - baseline() - Simple co-occurrence baseline - decay() - Decay model - tilles() - Tilles model model can run different parameters compared human data understand learning mechanisms best explain cross-situational word learning behavior.

title: XSLmodels 0.2.0
text: Added helper functions show_models(), show_datasets(), get_group_model_fit(), get_crossvalidated_model_fit() (now actually exported working) Re-implemented k-fold cross-validation (cross_validated_group_fits(), get_train_test_split()) current xslMod/xslData architecture Added 9 new conditions xsl_datasets (44 -> 53): 2 Kachergis, Yu, & Shiffrin (2009) (temporal contiguity), 3 Suanda, Mugwanya, & Namy (2014), 4 Koehne, Trueswell, & Gleitman (2013)

title: XSLmodels 0.2.0
text: xslData() longer rejects data response_matrix supplied – breaking vignette, function examples, get_example_ambiguous_condition()/get_example_unambiguous_condition() get_group_model_fit()/get_crossvalidated_model_fit() called uncfam_sampling(), multi_sampling(), tilles(), fazly() incorrect parameter names, causing error models update_params() dropping parameter list’s names/type, made xsl_run() error every model except bayesian_decay() whenever called fitting (e.g. xsl_fit() cross-validation) xsl_fit() now treats model erroring returning NA/NaN SSE given parameter draw infinitely bad fit instead aborting whole optimization run Fixed tilles() mislabeling model name "rescorla_wagner" create_cooc_matrix() test incorrect expected value plot_training_trials() now gives informative error optional viridis/gganimate packages aren’t installed, instead raw namespace-loading error xsl_run() checked !.null(dat$test) decide whether use mafc_test(), xslData()’s test defaults list() (NULL), dataset built without explicit test trials – including package’s get_example_ambiguous_condition()/get_example_unambiguous_condition() – silently routed mafc_test(mat, list()). returned numeric(0) rather erroring (R quirk: 1:0 counts instead empty, vec[] <- numeric(0) truncates vector rather erroring), producing false sse = 0 “perfect fit” warning. Now checks length(dat$test) > 0.
